name: Aasimar
description: Whether descended from a celestial being or infused with heavenly power, aasimar are mortals who carry a spark of the Upper Planes within their souls. This spark grants them a measure of the power of the Upper Planes and sets them apart from their fellow mortals.

traits.name: Celestial Resistance
traits.description: You have Resistance to Necrotic damage and Radiant damage.

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.description: You have Darkvision with a range of 60 feet.

traits.name: Healing Hands
traits.description: As a Magic action, you can touch a creature and roll a number of d4s equal to your Proficiency Bonus. The creature regains a number of Hit Points equal to the total rolled. Once you use this trait, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

traits.name: Light Bearer
traits.description: You know the Light cantrip. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for it.

traits.name: Celestial Revelation
traits.description: When you reach character level 3, you can transform as a Bonus Action using one of the options below (choose the option each time you transform). The transformation lasts for 1 minute or until you end it (no action required). Once you transform, you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest. Heavenly Wings: Spectral wings sprout from your back temporarily. Until the transformation ends, you have a Fly Speed equal to your Speed. Necrotic Shroud: Your eyes briefly become pools of darkness and Necrotic energy radiates from you. Until the transformation ends, each creature that is not a Construct or Undead that starts its turn within 10 feet of you must succeed on a Charisma saving throw (DC 8 + your Charisma modifier + your Proficiency Bonus) or have the Frightened condition until the start of its next turn. Inner Radiance: Searing light temporarily radiates from your eyes and hands. Until the transformation ends, you shed Bright Light in a 10-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 10 feet, and at the end of each of your turns each creature within 10 feet of you takes Radiant damage equal to your Proficiency Bonus.

name: Dragonborn
description: The ancestors of dragonborn hatched from the eggs of chromatic and metallic dragons. One story holds that these eggs were blessed by Bahamut and Tiamat, who wanted to populate the multiverse with people who embodied the virtues and vices of dragonkind. Whatever their origin, dragonborn have a proud lineage.

traits.name: Draconic Ancestry
traits.description: Your lineage stems from a dragon progenitor. Choose the kind of dragon from the Draconic Ancestry table. Your choice affects your Breath Weapon and Damage Resistance traits. The chosen dragon also affects your appearance, with your scales taking on a hue matching your dragon ancestor.

traits.name: Breath Weapon
traits.description: When you take the Attack action on your turn, you can replace one of your attacks with an exhalation of magical energy in either a 15-foot Cone or a 30-foot Line that is 5 feet wide (choose the shape each time). Each creature in that area must make a Dexterity saving throw (DC 8 + your Constitution modifier + your Proficiency Bonus). On a failed save, a creature takes 1d10 damage of the type associated with your Draconic Ancestry. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage. This damage increases by 1d10 when you reach character levels 5 (2d10), 11 (3d10), and 17 (4d10). You can use this Breath Weapon a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

traits.name: Damage Resistance
traits.description: You have Resistance to the damage type associated with your Draconic Ancestry.

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.description: You have Darkvision with a range of 60 feet.

traits.name: Draconic Flight
traits.description: When you reach character level 5, you can channel draconic magic to give yourself temporary flight. As a Bonus Action, you sprout spectral draconic wings on your back that last for 10 minutes or until you retract them (no action required). During that time, you have a Fly Speed equal to your Speed. Once you use this trait, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

name: Dwarf
description: Dwarves are found in mountains, hills, and underground vaults, where they mine for ore, craft exceptional objects, and defend their strongholds against monster attacks. Their culture values hard work, loyalty to kin, and devotion to the gods of the dwarves.

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.description: You have Darkvision with a range of 120 feet.

traits.name: Dwarven Resilience
traits.description: You have Resistance to Poison damage. You also have Advantage on saving throws you make to avoid or end the Poisoned condition.

traits.name: Dwarven Toughness
traits.description: Your Hit Point maximum increases by 1, and it increases by 1 again whenever you gain a level.

traits.name: Stonecunning
traits.description: As a Bonus Action, you gain Tremorsense with a range of 60 feet for 10 minutes. You must be on a stone surface or touching a stone surface to use this Tremorsense. The stone can be natural or worked. You can use this Bonus Action a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

name: Elf
description: Created by the god Corellon, the first elves could change their forms at will. They lost this ability after siding with Lolth during a war, but some gained a new power—foresight. The elves allied with Corellon gained the Feywild as a new home. Since then, elves have spread across the multiverse.

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.description: You have Darkvision with a range of 60 feet.

traits.name: Elven Lineage
traits.description: You are part of an elven lineage that grants you supernatural abilities. Choose a lineage from the Elven Lineages table: Drow (Underdark), High Elf (Surface), or Wood Elf (Forest). You gain the Level 1 benefit of that lineage. Starting at level 3 and again at level 5, you also gain the ability to cast a spell with this trait. Once you cast the spell with this trait, you can't cast that spell with it again until you finish a Long Rest; however, you can cast the spell using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells when you cast them with this trait (choose when you select this species).

traits.name: Fey Ancestry
traits.description: You have Advantage on saving throws you make to avoid or end the Charmed condition.

traits.name: Keen Senses
traits.description: You have proficiency in the Insight, Perception, or Survival skill (choose one).

traits.name: Trance
traits.description: You don't need to sleep, and magic can't put you to sleep. You can finish a Long Rest in 4 hours if you spend those hours in a trancelike meditation, during which you retain consciousness.

name: Gnome
description: Darting through forests and mountains, gnomes are small folk who are curious about the world and skilled in arcane, natural, and mechanical arts. Gnomes tend toward cleverness, tinkering, and a love of learning.

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.description: You have Darkvision with a range of 60 feet.

traits.name: Gnomish Cunning
traits.description: You have Advantage on Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma saving throws.

traits.name: Gnomish Lineage
traits.description: You are part of a gnomish lineage that grants you supernatural abilities. Choose a lineage: Forest Gnome or Rock Gnome. Forest Gnome: You know the Minor Illusion cantrip. Starting at level 3, you can cast the Speak with Animals spell with this trait. Rock Gnome: You know the Mending and Prestidigitation cantrips. Starting at level 3, you can cast the Identify spell with this trait. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells (choose when you select this species). When you cast these spells with this trait, they don't require material components.

name: Goliath
description: Towering over most folk, goliaths are distant kin of giants. Each goliath bears the favored blessing of one of the giant gods, which influences their temperament and grants them special abilities.

traits.name: Giant Ancestry
traits.description: You are descended from giants. Choose one of the following benefits—a supernatural boon from your giant heritage; you can use the chosen benefit a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest: Cloud's Jaunt (Cloud Giant): As a Bonus Action, you magically teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. Fire's Burn (Fire Giant): When you hit a target with an attack roll and deal damage, you can also deal 1d10 Fire damage to that target. Frost's Chill (Frost Giant): When you hit a target with an attack roll and deal damage, you can also deal 1d6 Cold damage to the target and reduce its Speed by 10 feet until the start of your next turn. Hill's Tumble (Hill Giant): When you hit a Large or smaller creature with an attack roll and deal damage, you can give that target the Prone condition. Stone's Endurance (Stone Giant): When you take damage, you can use your Reaction to roll 1d12. Add your Constitution modifier to the number rolled and reduce the damage you take by that total. Storm's Thunder (Storm Giant): When you take damage from a creature within 60 feet of you, you can use your Reaction to deal 1d8 Thunder damage to that creature.

traits.name: Large Form
traits.description: Starting at character level 5, you can change your size to Large as a Bonus Action if you're in a big enough space. This transformation lasts for 10 minutes or until you end it (no action required). For that duration, you have Advantage on Strength checks, your Speed increases by 10 feet, and you can use your Powerful Build trait. When the transformation ends, you return to your normal size. Once you use this trait, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

traits.name: Powerful Build
traits.description: You count as one size larger when determining your carrying capacity and the weight you can push, drag, or lift.

name: Halfling
description: Cherished and guided by gods who value life, halflings are a species characterized by optimism. They tend to be small in stature but large in spirit. They make their homes in cozy burrows and rustic villages, but many halflings set out on adventures.

traits.name: Brave
traits.description: You have Advantage on saving throws you make to avoid or end the Frightened condition.

traits.name: Halfling Nimbleness
traits.description: You can move through the space of any creature that is a size larger than you, but you can't stop in the same space.

traits.name: Luck
traits.description: When you roll a 1 on the d20 of a D20 Test, you can reroll the die, and you must use the new roll.

traits.name: Naturally Stealthy
traits.description: You can take the Hide action even when you are obscured only by a creature that is at least one size larger than you.

name: Human
description: Humans are the most common people in the worlds of D&D. In the cosmopolitan cities of the multiverse, humans jostle for position alongside dwarves, elves, and countless other species. Humans' extraordinary adaptability makes them standouts in any world they inhabit.

traits.name: Resourceful
traits.description: You gain Heroic Inspiration whenever you finish a Long Rest.

traits.name: Skillful
traits.description: You gain proficiency in one skill of your choice.

traits.name: Versatile
traits.description: You gain an Origin feat of your choice (see chapter 5). Skilled is recommended.

name: Orc
description: Orcs trace their creation to Gruumsh, a powerful god of slaughter and bloodshed who mourned that the elder races had claimed all the lands of the world when he arrived. He cursed his creations with a burning rage that they might one day reclaim what he thought should be theirs. Many orcs have abandoned the worship of Gruumsh but still feel his influence.

traits.name: Adrenaline Rush
traits.description: You can take the Dash action as a Bonus Action. You can use this trait a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Short or Long Rest. Whenever you use this trait, you gain a number of Temporary Hit Points equal to your Proficiency Bonus.

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.description: You have Darkvision with a range of 120 feet.

traits.name: Powerful Build
traits.description: You count as one size larger when determining your carrying capacity and the weight you can push, drag, or lift.

traits.name: Relentless Endurance
traits.description: When you are reduced to 0 Hit Points but not killed outright, you can drop to 1 Hit Point instead. Once you use this trait, you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest.

name: Tiefling
description: Tieflings are either born in the Lower Planes or have the essence of the Lower Planes woven into their being. Some tieflings are the direct offspring of a devil or a demon, while others descend from a mortal who made a diabolical bargain.

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.description: You have Darkvision with a range of 60 feet.

traits.name: Fiendish Legacy
traits.description: You are the recipient of a legacy that grants you supernatural abilities. Choose a legacy from the Fiendish Legacies table: Abyssal (Demons), Chthonic (Dark gods), or Infernal (Devils). You gain the level 1 benefit of the chosen legacy. Starting at level 3 and again at level 5, you also gain the ability to cast a spell with this trait. Once you cast the spell with this trait, you can't cast it again until you finish a Long Rest; however, you can cast it using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells (choose when you select this species).

traits.name: Otherworldly Presence
traits.description: You know the Thaumaturgy cantrip. When you cast it with this trait, the spell uses the same spellcasting ability you chose for your Fiendish Legacy trait.
